data.title: MRPeasy 2025: New Features That Actually Matter for Small Manufacturer
data.excerpt: MRPeasy just dropped game-changing updates for small manufacturers. See the new features, who benefits most, and why it's worth your time.

$media.alt: MRPeasy 2025: New Features That Actually Matter for Small Manufacturers

children.text: MRPeasy 2025: New Features That Actually Matter for Small Manufacturers
*I may earn a small commission if you sign up through links in this post, at no extra cost to you.*

There's a painful gap in manufacturing software. Enterprise ERPs cost a fortune and take months to implement. Spreadsheet hacks break the moment you hit 50 orders. And most "affordable" MRP tools feel like they were designed in 2008 and abandoned shortly after.

MRPeasy has been quietly closing that gap for years. Built specifically for manufacturers and distributors with 10 to 200 employees, it's the rare tool that feels both genuinely powerful and actually usable. No six-month onboarding. No consultant army required.

I've been following MRPeasy's evolution closely, and their recent updates deserve real attention. This isn't a "we changed the icon colors" kind of update. These are functional improvements that directly impact how small manufacturers run their operations daily.
Let me walk you through what's new, who benefits most, and whether it's worth your time.
What's New: MRPeasy's Latest Feature Updates
MRPeasy has rolled out a series of improvements that hit the areas small manufacturers care about most: planning speed, visibility, and integration depth.
AI-Powered MRP Calculations

This is the headline feature, and it's genuinely useful rather than gimmicky. MRPeasy now uses AI to optimize material requirements planning calculations. What does this mean in practice? The system analyzes your historical demand patterns, lead times, and current inventory to generate smarter purchasing and production recommendations.

Instead of manually reviewing every material requirement, the software flags exactly what you need to order, when, and in what quantities. For a 30-person shop juggling 200 SKUs, this alone can save hours every week.
Enhanced Drag-and-Drop Production Rescheduling

Production scheduling was already one of MRPeasy's strongest features. The updated drag-and-drop interface now includes real-time capacity visualization. You can see at a glance which workstations are overloaded, which have slack, and what happens to downstream orders when you move a job.

No more guessing. No more "let me check the spreadsheet and get back to you." You drag a production order to a new slot, and the system instantly recalculates material availability, labor allocation, and delivery dates.
Upgraded Internet-Kiosk Reporting

Shop floor reporting just got smoother. The Internet-Kiosk mode—designed for workers to log time and report progress from shared devices—now supports faster data entry and improved visual feedback. Workers see their assigned tasks clearly, can report quantities completed with fewer taps, and supervisors get real-time visibility into production status without interrupting anyone.
Deeper Integration Ecosystem
MRPeasy has expanded its integration lineup significantly. The platform now connects natively with:
QuickBooks Online and Xero
for seamless accounting sync
Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, and BigCommerce
for e-commerce order flow
ShipStation
for shipping logistics
Pipedrive
for CRM pipeline management
Microsoft Power BI
for advanced analytics
Zapier
for connecting to virtually anything else

The Shopify integration is particularly noteworthy. Sales orders flow directly into MRPeasy, triggering production orders or inventory reservations automatically. For manufacturers selling direct-to-consumer, this eliminates the manual order entry bottleneck that causes errors and delays.
If you want to see how these integrations work in practice, check out our
step-by-step MRPeasy tutorial
Who Is MRPeasy Ideal For?
MRPeasy isn't trying to be everything to everyone. That's actually what makes it so good at what it does. Here's who gets the most value:
Small Manufacturers (10–200 Employees)

This is MRPeasy's sweet spot. If you're a manufacturer that's outgrown spreadsheets but can't justify a $100K+ ERP implementation, this is your tool. Companies in electronics, food production, metal fabrication, cosmetics, textiles, and consumer goods all use MRPeasy successfully.

The software handles multi-level bills of materials, complex routing, and sub-contracted operations without requiring a dedicated IT team to maintain it. One user put it perfectly: "Best value in the small manufacturing space by far."
Growing Distributors

If you manage inventory across multiple warehouses and need tight coordination between purchasing, storage, and order fulfillment, MRPeasy delivers. The warehouse management system supports multiple locations, bin tracking, and serial/lot number traceability.
Teams That Need Compliance Documentation

MRPeasy's lot traceability and documentation features are strong enough that ISO auditors love them. Multiple users report that their auditors were impressed by how thoroughly the system documents everything. If you're pursuing ISO 9001, ISO 13485, or similar certifications, MRPeasy builds compliance into your daily workflow rather than forcing you to maintain separate documentation systems.
Operations Managers Who've Hit the Spreadsheet Wall

You know the signs: your BOM spreadsheet has 47 tabs. Nobody knows the real inventory count. Production planning is a weekly fire drill. Purchase orders get placed too late, and you're constantly explaining delays to customers.

MRPeasy solves all of this in one integrated system. And you don't need to be a tech expert to use it. The interface is clean enough that your shop floor workers can log in and report production without calling IT for help.
Want to explore specific use cases? Our
MRPeasy use cases guide
breaks down real scenarios.
Why MRPeasy Stands Out in the MRP Software Market
I've reviewed dozens of manufacturing software tools. Here's what makes MRPeasy genuinely different:
It's Actually Easy to Use

This sounds like marketing fluff, but it's not. MRPeasy earned "easiest to use" recognition from G2 and consistently ranks high on Capterra for usability. The onboarding process takes days, not months. Most teams are running real production through the system within two weeks.
That speed matters. Every week your team spends implementing software is a week they're not focused on production.
The Price Is Honest

MRPeasy starts at $49 per user per month on the Starter plan and $69 per user per month on the Professional plan. Annual billing gets you a free month. There are no hidden modules, no "enterprise unlock" fees, and no surprise charges for basic features.

Compare that to enterprise ERPs that quote $50K–$200K upfront plus ongoing maintenance. For a 25-person manufacturer, MRPeasy costs roughly $1,500–$2,000 per month total. That's less than a single part-time hire.
You can start a free trial right now
and see the difference yourself. No credit card required.
Real Users Actually Recommend It

MRPeasy consistently earns "Users Love Us" and "Best ROI" badges on G2. Over 2,000 manufacturers trust the platform. One reviewer with 15 years of software experience called it "much better than the industry average" in terms of reliability. Another reported that their production capacity doubled after implementation—not because they added equipment, but because MRPeasy eliminated the planning chaos that was wasting their existing capacity.
That's the kind of result that gets my attention. Not "we saved 5%" but "we doubled what we could produce with the same resources."
For a deeper look at why manufacturers choose this platform, read our analysis on
why choose MRPeasy
How MRPeasy Compares to Other Productivity Tools
You might be wondering: "I already use tools like
ClickUp
or
monday.com
. Do I really need a dedicated MRP?"
The honest answer: yes, if you're manufacturing physical products.

General project management tools are excellent for tracking tasks, deadlines, and team communication. But they don't understand bills of materials. They don't calculate material requirements based on demand forecasts. They don't track lot numbers for compliance. They don't integrate purchasing with production scheduling.

MRPeasy is purpose-built for the complexity of manufacturing operations. It does things that general productivity tools simply can't, like automatically generating purchase orders when inventory drops below reorder points, or calculating production timelines based on actual machine capacity and labor availability.

That said, MRPeasy plays well with other tools. You can connect it to your existing stack via native integrations or Zapier. It complements rather than replaces your CRM, project management, and communication tools.
Pricing and Getting Started
MRPeasy keeps pricing simple:
| Plan | Price | Best For |
Starter
| $49/user/month | Small teams getting started with MRP |
Professional
| $69/user/month | Growing manufacturers needing full features |
Enterprise
| Custom pricing | Larger operations with advanced requirements |
All plans include a free trial. Annual billing saves you one month. Users beyond the initial tier are added at $79 per 10 users.

The Starter plan alone includes production planning, BOM management, lot traceability, supply chain management, warehouse management, workforce planning, CRM, standard accounting, multi-language and multi-currency support, and all integrations. That's a staggering amount of functionality at the entry level.
Many competing tools gate basic features behind expensive tiers. MRPeasy doesn't.
Our
MRPeasy tips and best practices
will help you get maximum value from whichever plan you choose.
Real Results: What Users Report
Numbers talk, but user stories talk louder. Here's what MRPeasy customers consistently report:
Capacity improvements.
Multiple users describe significant increases in production output after implementation. Not from adding machines—from eliminating scheduling waste and reducing stockouts.
Time savings.
Production planning that used to take a full day now takes an hour. Purchase order generation is automated. Inventory counts are always current.
Audit readiness.
Companies pursuing ISO certification report that auditors are impressed by MRPeasy's built-in documentation. Everything is traceable, from raw material lots to finished goods shipments.
Scalability.
Teams that started with MRPeasy at 10 employees continue using it at 80+ without outgrowing the system. The platform scales with your business.
Reliability.
In an industry where buggy software is the norm, MRPeasy stands out for stability. One long-time user described it as "impressively bug-free" compared to industry averages.
The Bottom Line

MRPeasy isn't trying to replace SAP for Fortune 500 companies. It's doing something arguably harder: giving small manufacturers real ERP functionality without the enterprise price tag, complexity, or implementation timeline.

The 2025 updates—AI-powered MRP calculations, enhanced scheduling visualization, improved shop floor reporting, and deeper integrations—show that MRPeasy is actively investing in the features that matter most to its users.
If you're a manufacturer or distributor with 10–200 employees and you're still relying on spreadsheets, disconnected tools, or an outdated system that nobody likes, MRPeasy deserves a serious look.
Start your free MRPeasy trial today
. It takes five minutes to sign up, and you'll know within a week whether it's the right fit. In my experience, for the small manufacturing space, nothing else comes close.

Frequently Asked Questions
Is MRPeasy suitable for a manufacturer with fewer than 10 employees?

Absolutely. While MRPeasy is optimized for the 10–200 employee range, many smaller shops use it successfully. The Starter plan at $49/user/month is affordable even for a 3-person operation. If you're managing more than 50 SKUs and multiple production orders, MRPeasy will save you time regardless of team size.
How long does it take to implement MRPeasy?

Most teams are operational within 1–2 weeks. The onboarding wizard guides you through entering products, BOMs, and suppliers. You don't need a consultant or IT specialist. MRPeasy's support team—consistently rated as some of the best in the industry—helps with any questions during setup.
Can MRPeasy handle multi-currency and multi-language operations?

Yes. All plans include multi-currency and multi-language support as standard features. This makes MRPeasy a strong choice for manufacturers with international suppliers or customers. You can operate in your preferred language while your team in another country uses theirs.
Does MRPeasy replace QuickBooks or Xero?

MRPeasy includes standard accounting features, but most users integrate it with QuickBooks Online or Xero rather than replacing their accounting software. The native integration syncs invoices, purchase orders, and financial transactions automatically. You get manufacturing-specific accounting within MRPeasy while your accountant continues working in their preferred platform.
What happens to my data if I cancel?

MRPeasy allows you to export your data at any time. Your information belongs to you. There are no hostage tactics or data lock-in practices. You can export products, BOMs, inventory records, transaction history, and reports in standard formats.
